description: Medium: Preserve If-None-Match: * POST semantics when a concurrent create wins after the in-session absence check.
acceptance-criteria: A relational POST that selected CreateNew under If-None-Match: * maps a root-natural-key uniqueness race to 412 Precondition Failed instead of an identity conflict or generic write conflict.
A descriptor POST that selected CreateNew under If-None-Match: * maps the equivalent descriptor uniqueness race to 412 Precondition Failed.
Constraint failures for requests without If-None-Match: * and constraint failures unrelated to the guarded target retain their existing mappings.
Deterministic tests cover the losing concurrent-create outcome for ordinary resources and descriptors without weakening the database uniqueness guard.
steps-to-verify: Review the root-natural-key branch in RelationalWriteDatabaseFailureResultMapper and the descriptor unique-constraint catch to confirm that only a CreateNew POST guarded by If-None-Match: * receives the precondition-failure mapping.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~IfNoneMatch'.

Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~WriteCreateGuardIfNoneMatch' with both PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration databases configured.

description: Medium: Enforce the mandatory GET-by-id ETag invariant instead of silently returning 200 without an ETag header.
acceptance-criteria: GetByIdHandler requires every successful repository result to contain a non-empty string _etag before constructing a 200 or 304 response.
A successful repository result with a missing, empty, or non-string _etag fails with an actionable invariant error and cannot produce a 200 response without an ETag header.
Normal 200 and matching If-None-Match 304 responses continue to use the same served tag in the response header.
Unit coverage includes the missing-etag invariant as well as the existing 200 and 304 success cases.

The cross-engine profiled GET scenario no longer claims GET omits ETag; it asserts that the real HTTP 200 response header is present, equals the body _etag, and can be used for the subsequent If-Match write.
An E2E GET-by-id scenario explicitly asserts the ETag response header after the GET, rather than relying only on the POST header or the body _etag.
steps-to-verify: Review GetByIdHandler.CreateSuccessResponse and confirm that it no longer uses an empty-string fallback for the etag response header.
Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~GetByIdHandler'.

Run dotnet test src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~ResponseHeader'.

Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~ProfileRootOnlyMerge_ProfiledHttp' with both PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration databases configured.
Run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e' -TestFilter 'Category=@e2e-ci-shard-1'.

description: Medium: Add real PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and E2E coverage for descriptor If-None-Match behavior.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL descriptor integration tests cover guarded POST insert, guarded POST-to-existing 412, existing PUT 412, and missing PUT 404 behavior.
SQL Server descriptor integration tests cover the same guarded POST and PUT outcomes.
At least one cross-engine HTTP-pipeline descriptor scenario proves that a GET emits ETag and a matching If-None-Match returns 304 with no body.
A Docker-stack E2E descriptor scenario covers a matching conditional GET and wildcard create-guard behavior through the public descriptor endpoints.
The descriptor tests exercise the real DescriptorWriteHandler and descriptor read path rather than fake command executors.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~DescriptorWriteHandler'.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~DescriptorWriteHandler' with ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin configured.
Run the descriptor conditional-GET scenario through src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj for both database fixtures.
Run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e' -TestFilter '<descriptor If-None-Match scenario filter>'.

description: Low: Remove stale If-Match-only and single-tag leftovers and make generalized ETag precondition dispatch explicit.
acceptance-criteria: The unused EtagValue.TryParseConditionalTag method and its single-tag-only tests are removed, and comments point directly to ParseConditionalTagList.
RelationalCurrentEtagPreconditionCheckResult no longer carries a CurrentEtag value that production callers never consume.
ExecutionStateResolutionOptions uses an ETag-precondition name instead of DeferredIfMatch for the path shared by If-Match and If-None-Match.

HasEtagPrecondition and EtagPreconditionEvaluator explicitly handle None, IfMatch, and IfNoneMatch, and reject an unknown future WritePrecondition arm instead of silently treating it as absent or satisfied.
Deferred precondition evaluation uses the same explicit precondition predicate rather than admitting every non-None arm.
The cleanup does not change conditional GET comparison, write-precondition polarity, or response status behavior.
steps-to-verify: Run rg 'TryParseConditionalTag|DeferredIfMatch|\.CurrentEtag' src/dms and confirm no stale production references remain.
Review the HasEtagPrecondition and EtagPreconditionEvaluator switches and confirm their fallback rejects unknown precondition types.
Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~EtagValue'.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~EtagPrecondition'.

description: Medium: Return If-None-Match-specific 412 Problem Details instead of describing the failure as an If-Match optimistic-lock conflict.
acceptance-criteria: A matching specific If-None-Match tag and If-None-Match: * against an existing POST or PUT target return status 412 with detail, title, type, and errors that identify the If-None-Match precondition and do not claim the item was modified by another user.
The corrected response shape is used by both ordinary-resource and descriptor write paths.
Existing If-Match mismatch and missing-target Problem Details remain unchanged.
Core handler or HTTP-pipeline tests assert the complete If-None-Match 412 response body rather than only the status code.
steps-to-verify: Review ETagPreconditionFailureReason, FailureResponse, and the regular and descriptor precondition-failure mappings to confirm that If-Match and If-None-Match select distinct response semantics.

Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~UpsertHandler|FullyQualifiedName~UpdateByIdHandler'.

Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~WriteCreateGuardIfNoneMatch' with both PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration databases configured.

description: Low: Consolidate duplicated conditional-GET unit fixtures and E2E If-None-Match request helpers.
acceptance-criteria: GetByIdHandler conditional-GET cases reuse one successful repository fixture and parameterized inputs instead of repeating the same repository, response body, setup, and assertions for every tag form.
The consolidated cases retain coverage for quoted, unquoted, weak, list hit, list miss, variant-only mismatch, bare wildcard, and quoted wildcard inputs.
The E2E If-None-Match GET step reuses the existing generic GET-with-header request path, including ETag placeholder resolution, instead of maintaining a second copy of URL and header construction.
The refactor preserves the repository testing style required by the project and does not reduce behavior coverage.
steps-to-verify: Review GetByIdHandlerTests.cs and confirm that the repeated nested Repository implementations and identical Setup/assert blocks have been removed.
Review StepDefinitions.cs and confirm there is one shared GET-with-header request implementation and one shared authenticated-header builder.
Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~GetByIdHandler'.
Run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e' -TestFilter 'FullyQualifiedName~ETag'.

description: Low: Correct the RFC 9110 section references introduced in the ETag ADR and If-None-Match comments.
acceptance-criteria: Weak-versus-strong validator text cites RFC 9110 section 8.8.1 and entity-tag comparison text cites section 8.8.3.2 where applicable.
Entity-tag grammar and etagc references cite RFC 9110 section 8.8.3 rather than sections 2.1 or 2.3.
If-Match behavior and wildcard references cite RFC 9110 section 13.1.1 rather than section 3.1, while If-None-Match and precondition precedence continue to cite sections 13.1.2 and 13.2.2.
Production comments, test comments, and the ADR use consistent current-RFC citations without changing behavior.
steps-to-verify: Run rg 'RFC 9110 §(2\.1|2\.3|3\.1)' reference/adr-etag-from-content-version.md src/dms and inspect any remaining matches for correctness.
Compare the resulting citations with https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#section-8.8 and https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#section-13.1.
Run git diff --check.

description: Medium: Parse If-None-Match entity-tag lists without treating commas inside quoted opaque tags as list delimiters.
acceptance-criteria: ParseConditionalTagList preserves a comma inside a quoted entity-tag as part of that tag and splits only between list members.
Quoted and unquoted DMS tags, weak W/ tags, optional whitespace, empty input, and sole bare-wildcard handling retain their current behavior.
A legal non-matching quoted entity-tag whose opaque value contains the current DMS tag between commas cannot cause a false 304 on GET or a false 412 on POST or PUT.
Unit tests cover quoted comma-containing tags alongside ordinary multi-tag lists and weak tags.
steps-to-verify: Review EtagValue.ParseConditionalTagList and confirm that it recognizes entity-tag quote boundaries instead of using string.Split(',').
Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~EtagValue|FullyQualifiedName~GetByIdHandler'.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~EtagPrecondition'.
Run git diff --check.
